name: Todo App
summary: Tiny example tree — a toy todo service

children.name: createTodo
children.summary: Creates a todo from the request body
children.explanation: HTTP POST handler that validates the incoming payload, persists a new todo, and returns it as JSON with a 201 status.
steps: Parse and validate the JSON body (title required, non-empty)
On invalid input, respond 400 with the validation error
Call todoRepo.insert() to persist the new row
Return the created todo as JSON with status 201

children.name: listTodos
children.summary: Lists todos with optional status filter
children.explanation: HTTP GET handler that returns all todos, optionally filtered by a ?status= query parameter.
steps: Read the optional status query parameter
If present, pass it as a filter to todoRepo.list()
Otherwise list all todos
Serialize the array to JSON and respond 200

children.name: validateTodoInput
children.explanation: Checks an untrusted request body against the Todo shape and returns either the cleaned input or a list of field errors.
steps: Assert the body is a non-null object
Require a non-empty string title, trimming whitespace
Coerce the optional status against the allowed set
Return { ok, value } or { ok:false, errors }

children.name: isStatus
children.summary: Type guard for the status enum
children.explanation: Narrows an arbitrary string to the TodoStatus union so callers can trust it downstream.
steps: Test membership in the STATUSES tuple
Return the boolean type-predicate result

children.name: insert
children.summary: Inserts a todo and returns it with an id
children.explanation: Writes a new todo row to the database and returns the persisted record including its generated id and timestamps.
steps: Generate a UUID and createdAt timestamp
Run the parameterized INSERT statement
Map the returned row to a Todo object
Return the persisted Todo

children.name: list
children.explanation: Reads todos from the database, applying a status filter when one is provided, ordered newest first.
steps: Build the base SELECT ordered by createdAt DESC
If a status filter is given, append a WHERE clause and bind it
Execute the query
Map each row to a Todo and return the array

children.name: query
children.explanation: Thin wrapper over the connection pool that executes a parameterized statement and returns the resulting rows.
steps: Acquire a client from the pool
Execute the SQL with bound parameters
Release the client in a finally block
Return the rows array
